metadata.seed: I hate looking at myself in the mirror, every time I do I feel like the ugliest person in the whole world. I feel like nobody will ever love me.

patient.query: I hate looking at myself in the mirror, every time I do I feel like the ugliest person in the whole world. I feel like nobody will ever love me.

automatic_thoughts: I am the ugliest person in the whole world
nobody will ever love me

neutral: Does not recognize self in mirror (finding)
Own reflection in mirror seems unreal (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like looking in the mirror is really painful for you. What might someone who knows you well think about how you look?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really difficult to look in the mirror and feel that way. That must be incredibly painful for you.

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds really difficult. What makes that thought feel true to you?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I am the ugliest person in the whole world
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like looking in the mirror is really painful for you. What might someone who knows you well think about how you look?

safety_check.response:
**FINAL RESPONSE:**  
I hear how painful it must be to look in the mirror and feel that way. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: Yeah, it is painful. I guess if someone who knows me well were to say something, they might think I'm being too hard on myself. But honestly, I feel like they see something different than I do. Sometimes, I just wish I could feel what they see.

automatic_thoughts: I'm being too hard on myself
They see something different than I do
I wish I could feel what they see

neutral: Able to recognize own emotional pain and distress (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really difficult to feel understood, especially when you're struggling with these thoughts. Can you tell me more about what it feels like when you think others see things differently?

patient.query: I don't know, I guess it’s just how they act around me. Like, when they compliment me, it feels almost like they're trying to make me feel better or something. I can't shake the feeling that they’re being nice, but they don't really mean it. It all just feels so... disconnected.

automatic_thoughts: they're trying to make me feel better
they don't really mean it
it feels disconnected

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling quite disconnected when people try to be kind, and there's a part of you that wonders if their intentions are genuine. That must be really challenging for you.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're feeling quite disconnected when people try to be kind, and there's a part of you that wonders if their intentions are genuine. That must be really challenging for you.

socratic_questioning.response: What makes you feel that way?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like those moments can feel really distant and uncertain for you. What might a trusted friend say about why they might offer that kind of kindness?

safety_check.response: It sounds like those moments of disconnection are really weighing on you. Can you tell me more about how those feelings impact your sense of connection or understanding?

patient.query: Yeah, it is. It’s like, I want to believe them, but there's this voice in my head saying they're just saying it to be polite or avoid awkwardness. I guess I feel a little lost because I want to feel good about myself, but it’s hard when I doubt what others see. It’s exhausting, really.

automatic_thoughts: they're just saying it to be polite
they're avoiding awkwardness
I doubt what others see

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: How does that feeling of being misunderstood affect your interactions with others?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're caught between trusting others and doubting their intentions, which is really wearing you down. That exhaustion makes sense when you're constantly questioning how others perceive you.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Automatic thoughts: they're just saying it to be polite, they're avoiding awkwardness, I doubt what others see
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

socratic_questioning.response: How does that feeling of being misunderstood affect your interactions with others?

alternative_perspective.response: That inner voice can be tricky. Have you ever asked a friend or trusted person what they think about this situation?

safety_check.response: It sounds really draining to carry that doubt around, especially when you're already feeling exhausted. Can you tell me more about how that inner voice influences your thoughts?

patient.query: It makes me really hesitant to open up. I find myself second-guessing how I present myself, and I tend to hold back a lot in conversations. It feels like I’m always trying to measure how much of me I can show without being judged or seen in the wrong light. It's just... tiring, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I might be judged
I need to hold back
I can't show my true self

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds really draining to feel like you're always measuring how much of yourself you can show. Can you tell me how holding back affects you?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really draining to feel like you're always measuring how much of yourself you can show. Can you tell me how holding back affects you?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you're feeling hesitant because you're constantly measuring how much of yourself you can show. Can you tell me more about how you decide what to reveal or hold back?

alternative_perspective.response: It makes sense that you'd feel hesitant when you're worried about being judged. Have you ever thought about how a trusted friend might describe your interactions?

safety_check.response: It sounds really difficult to navigate those conversations, and I can see why that would feel so draining. You're clearly putting a lot of effort into managing how others perceive you, which must be emotionally taxing.

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely affects how I connect with people. I feel like I’m missing out on getting to know them better, and they’re missing out on me too. I keep thinking that if I reveal too much, they’ll see the flaws I see, and then it’ll just be awkward or they’ll pull away. So I kind of just... stay quiet, I guess.

automatic_thoughts: they'll see the flaws I see
it'll be awkward
they'll pull away

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: That makes sense. When you stay quiet, how do you think that affects your ability to connect with people?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're worried that revealing too much might make others see your flaws, leading to awkwardness or rejection. That makes sense, and it's completely understandable why that would make you choose to stay quiet.

socratic_questioning.response: That makes sense. When you stay quiet, how do you think that affects your ability to connect with people?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like staying quiet helps you feel safer, but I wonder what you think a really trusted friend would say about your worries?

retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're worried that revealing too much might lead to awkwardness or rejection, and that staying quiet helps you avoid that discomfort. Can you tell me how staying quiet affects your relationships?

patient.query: It feels lonely, honestly. I want to be part of things, but when I don’t speak up, I feel like I’m just on the outside looking in. Sometimes, I think they might see me as distant or uninterested, but really, I just feel scared. It’s frustrating because I don’t want to be alone, but I don’t know how to break that cycle.

automatic_thoughts: they might see me as distant
they might see me as uninterested
I don’t know how to break that cycle

entailment: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: That sounds really difficult. When you say you feel scared, what happens when you don't speak up?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really tough to feel like you're on the outside, especially when you're hoping to connect with others. Can you tell me more about how staying silent affects your sense of belonging?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of being in a small group (finding)
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds really difficult. When you say you feel scared, what happens when you don't speak up?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds really tough to feel that way. Have you ever thought about what a trusted friend might say if they saw how you're feeling?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: fear of being in a small group
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

safety_check.response: It sounds really tough to be feeling lonely and stuck in that cycle, especially when you're craving connection. Can you tell me more about what keeps you from speaking up?

retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: they might see me as uninterested
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I don't know how to break that cycle
retrieved_concepts_used.label: unknown

patient.query: When I don’t say anything, my mind races with all these thoughts. I start imagining what they think of me, and I feel this knot in my stomach. Then, it’s like I just freeze up, and all the things I want to say just get stuck. It feels like I’m trapped in my own head.

automatic_thoughts: what they think of me
I’m trapped in my own head

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of speaking to people in authority (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like staying silent can really trigger a lot of internal chaos for you, with thoughts racing and feelings of being trapped taking over.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like staying silent can really trigger a lot of internal chaos for you, with thoughts racing and feelings of being trapped taking over.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like those thoughts can feel overwhelming and stuck in your head. Can you tell me more about how they feel when they come up?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like those thoughts are really overwhelming you, making it hard to speak. Have you ever thought about how a close friend might describe this experience?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I’m trapped in my own head
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

safety_check.response: When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: Yeah, it does. It’s like I’m in this bubble, and I can see everyone else moving and connecting, but I can’t get out. I want to share my thoughts, but then I get worried about how they'll react. It feels like every little thing I might say has the potential to just... blow up in my face, you know?

automatic_thoughts: worried about reactions
everything I say could blow up

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling really stuck, like you're watching others connect easily while you're left out, and the worry about how your words might be received is really weighing on you.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're feeling really stuck, like you're watching others connect easily while you're left out, and the worry about how your words might be received is really weighing on you.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: fear of saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

socratic_questioning.response: That bubble metaphor really captures how you feel stuck and separate from others. Can you tell me more about what it feels like when you try to connect?

alternative_perspective.response: That's understandable—when you feel like you're stuck inside your own head, it can feel overwhelming. Have you ever thought about what a trusted friend might notice or say about how you're feeling right now?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're feeling really stuck and worried about how your words might be received, which is understandable when you're anxious about being misunderstood or judged.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: fear of saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

patient.query: Yeah, that’s exactly it. It’s frustrating and kind of heartbreaking at the same time. I want to be part of those connections, but this anxiety just holds me back. It feels so unfair, like I'm the only one who has to deal with this. Sometimes, I just wish it would all disappear.

automatic_thoughts: this anxiety holds me back
it's unfair
I'm the only one dealing with this
I wish it would all disappear

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds really tough to feel that way, especially when you're struggling with anxiety and wishing you could connect more easily. It makes sense that you'd feel frustrated and heartbroken when you're unable to be part of something you want to be.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds really tough to feel that way, especially when you're struggling with anxiety and wishing you could connect more easily. It makes sense that you'd feel frustrated and heartbroken when you're unable to be part of something you want to be.

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds really difficult—when you say it feels unfair and isolating, how does that sense of being alone affect your day-to-day life?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I'm the only one dealing with this
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I wish it would all disappear
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds really tough to deal with that anxiety, especially when you're trying to connect with others. Have you ever thought about whether others might feel the same way or have similar challenges?

safety_check.response: I hear how much that frustration and heartbreak sting, especially when you're longing to connect but held back by anxiety. When you mentioned wishing things would disappear, I wanted to gently check in—were you having any thoughts at all about not wanting to be here?
